title: End-to-end pilot corpus for one cancer subdomain
context: M0's capstone: run the entire pipeline end-to-end on one tractable cancer subdomain, producing a license-segregated, provenanced, datasheeted pilot corpus and releasing it to a real beneficiary (informal consumer or Zenodo DOI). All upstream M0 tasks must be complete: the canonical record model (001), the gate checklist (002), the versioned inclusion query (003), the license resolver (004), the bundle builder (005), the outreach/release path (006), and the named reviewer (014). This is the first proof that the pipeline actually works at a real scale and produces an accepted, citable output.
objective: Build an end-to-end pilot corpus for one chosen cancer subdomain: run the inclusion query, apply the license + cancer-guardrail gate, build license-segregated bundles, produce a datasheet, and release/submit it via the M0 acceptance path — with all gate artifacts committed and a confirmed acceptance evidence artifact recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Pilot subdomain chosen for tractable size and strong CC-BY coverage, on a realistic acceptance path; the choice is documented with rationale.

Every article in the pilot passed gate-002 with a committed tier/guardrail audit artifact; 0 tier leakage (no non-commercial/no-license article in the commercial bundle) and 0 patient-content leakage in redistributable bundles.
License correctness audited on a stratified sample (target 100% for the pilot); provenance is 100% complete on every included record.
Build is deterministic: manifest content hash recorded and reproduces on a clean rebuild from the same pinned snapshot.
Datasheet (Datasheet-for-Datasets format: composition, collection method, uses, limitations, guardrails) produced and committed.

Release accepted via informal channel or a published Zenodo DOI with the Steward's acceptance evidence artifact (outcomes/<release-id>.json) recorded in the repo — or submitted with the specific blocker surfaced.
License+compliance reviewer (pmc-cancer-reviewer-014) has signed off on the release before it ships.
output: A versioned pilot corpus release: manifest JSON + license-segregated bundles + per-bundle LICENSE manifests + datasheet + committed gate audit artifact + acceptance evidence artifact (outcomes/<release-id>.json), with manifest content hash recorded and the release accepted or submitted.
